text: Standing at the threshold of this modern, glass-walled room, everything feels clinical and deliberately designed—a space shaped by intention and order. As I move closer and enter, the colorful exhibition wall invites me to ponder the nature of innovation and the intersection of artifice and reality. I feel an odd sense of curiosity and anticipation, like I am both visitor and participant in an ongoing experiment. The glow of digital displays and art installations inspires a quiet awe, urging me to reflect on human creativity and our relationship with technology. Here, I am both observer and observed, my thoughts drifting between wonder, introspection, and subtle excitement.
